summary: Soundboard capture of the chaotic Woodstock set with a monster Turn On Your Lovelight and mixed performance quality.
review: The recording is a sound‑board feed that, despite occasional microphone hiss and power glitches, delivers an exceptionally clear picture of the August 16 1969 Woodstock show. Musically the band swings between brilliance – a near‑40‑minute Turn On Your Lovelight and a memorable Dark Star – and rough spots such as the aborted St. Stephen and general stage confusion.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise the historic performance and the soundboard’s clarity, but many note technical mishaps and uneven playing; weighted average of show (≈3) and recording (≈4) yields ~3.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear soundboard feed with occasional hum, microphone feedback and power‑out glitches; audience mix is well balanced, giving a vivid live feel.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical Dead set hampered by on‑stage technical chaos; the first half feels distracted, while the second half builds into a legendary Lovelight jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Suffered a microphone incident that knocked him back and bruised his lip, which impacted his playing early in the set.
